---
title: "Error handlers"
description: Customize the handling of the errors originating from the app.
---

# Error handlers

Customize the handling of the errors originating from the app.

import NpmLogo from '@site/static/img/npm-logo.png';

  <div class="col">

      <span class="margin-right--xs">@dynatrace-sdk/error-handlers</span>

```bash
npm install @dynatrace-sdk/error-handlers
```

## Interfaces

### CommonSerializedError

<!-- default child "Interface" -->

Common serialized JS Error

#### Properties

| Name                         | Type                                                                                     |
| ---------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| cause                        | <a href="https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/2/everyday-types.html#any">any</a> |
| details                      | <a href="https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/2/everyday-types.html#any">any</a> |
| errorRef                     | <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String">string</a>            |
| message<sup>\*required</sup> | <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String">string</a>            |
| name<sup>\*required</sup>    | <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String">string</a>            |
| stack                        | <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String">string</a>            |
| type<sup>\*required</sup>    | COMMON                                                                                   |

### HttpSerializedError

<!-- default child "Interface" -->

Serialized http exception with response status

#### Properties

| Name                         | Type                                                                                     |
| ---------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| body                         | unknown                                                                                  |
| cause                        | <a href="https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/2/everyday-types.html#any">any</a> |
| details                      | <a href="https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/2/everyday-types.html#any">any</a> |
| errorRef                     | <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String">string</a>            |
| message<sup>\*required</sup> | <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String">string</a>            |
| name<sup>\*required</sup>    | <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String">string</a>            |
| stack                        | <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String">string</a>            |
| status<sup>\*required</sup>  | <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number">number</a>            |
| type<sup>\*required</sup>    | HTTP                                                                                     |

## Functions

### addGlobalErrorSerializer

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>addGlobalErrorSerializer(serializer): void</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Registers a global error serializer for handling uncatched errors.

</div>

#### Parameters

| Name                            | Type                  | Description                                                            |
| ------------------------------- | --------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| serializer<sup>\*required</sup> | GlobalErrorSerializer | Asynchronous function which serializes error if it match the condition |

<!-- no returns -->

</div>

### isGlobalErrorSerializerSupported

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>isGlobalErrorSerializerSupported(): <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean">boolean</a></strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Checks if global error serializer is supported.

</div>

<!-- no parameters -->

#### Returns

information if global error serializer is supported on current environment.

</div>
